mission.title: Install and Configure Kagent on Kubernetes
mission.description: kagent is a Kubernetes native framework for building AI agents, allowing you to easily deploy and manage AI workloads on Kubernetes. Installing kagent enables you to leverage its capabilities for developing intelligent applications in a cloud-native environment.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the kagent pods running in the 'kagent' namespace, and you will be able to access the service through port 8080. You should also see the resource limits applied to the kagent deployment.

uninstall.title: Remove kagent Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall kagent, delete the deployed resources and the namespace. Run the following commands:

upgrade.description: To upgrade kagent, apply the latest manifests. Ensure to specify the new version in the URLs. For example:

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If the kagent pods are not starting, check the pod logs for errors using the command: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagent`.

troubleshooting.title: CRDs Not Found
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter issues related to CRDs, ensure that they were applied successfully by running: `kubectl get crd -n kagent`.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: If the service is not accessible, verify that the service is running and check the endpoints with: `kubectl get endpoints -n kagent`.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
